evidence_slots.state_condition: When AES register reads select addr[6:2] values corresponding to key_big[191:0] or inter_state[127:0], rdata returns those values; no read-active authorization condition is checked locally.
evidence_slots.impact: Any software-visible requester that can reach AES MMIO can read AES key material and internal AES intermediate state.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI managers including core/debug masters
evidence_slots.object: AXI subordinate address regions and protected peripherals
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Access from managers to subordinate address regions should be mediated by privilege level, connectivity map, address-range checks, and security attributes; privilege must be consistently applied to read and write channels.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: axi_node_intf_wrap instantiates connectivity_mapping, which indexes access_ctrl_i by priv_lvl_i to generate s_connectivity_map. The generated connectivity map and address start/end rules are passed into axi_node for routing. The harness derives access_ctrl from ROM2 key_reg_out via access_ctrl_reg and supplies current core priv_lvl. This shows intended privilege/connectivity gating path, although enforcement internals inside axi_node are not shown in detail.
evidence_slots.path: priv_lvl_processor -> priv_lvl -> axi_node_intf_wrap.priv_lvl_i; ROM2 secure_reg -> access_ctrl_reg -> access_ctrl_i; connectivity_mapping -> s_connectivity_map -> axi_node routing
evidence_slots.state_condition: For each subordinate/manager pair, connectivity_map_o[i][j] = access_ctrl_i[i][j][priv_lvl_i] with special j==6 condition; AW/AR/W channels are routed through axi_node with connectivity map and address rules.
evidence_slots.impact: Visible wrapper does apply privilege-derived connectivity metadata to the routing block, reducing concern that priv_lvl is merely unused annotation.

evidence_slots.subject: APB/AXI requester reaching rom2
evidence_slots.object: rom2 memory contents, secure_reg key storage, jtag_key/access_ctrl derived values, and pkey_loc_o/key-location related data
evidence_slots.expected_guard: ROM-like protected contents should ignore or reject writes and should only expose intended read data for authorized address ranges; any pkey/key-location output should be mediated if sensitive.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: rom2 writes secure_reg[addr] <= wdata_i when req_i && we_i. rdata_o returns secure_reg[raddr_q]. ariane_peripherals connects rom2 through axi2mem, exports secure_reg as key_reg_out, assigns jtag_key and access_ctrl_reg from it. pkey_loc_o exists and is connected to a packet/peripheral-key-table area, but its exact sensitivity/use is not fully shown.
evidence_slots.path: AXI rom2_fuse -> axi2mem i_axi2rom2 -> rom2 req/we/addr/wdata/rdata -> secure_reg/key_reg_out -> jtag_key/access_ctrl_reg
evidence_slots.state_condition: On reset secure_reg copies const mem. When req_i is asserted and we_i is high, selected secure_reg entry is overwritten; when req_i and !we_i, raddr_q updates and rdata_o exposes selected secure_reg entry.
evidence_slots.impact: Bus requesters that can reach ROM2 can alter AES/JTAG keys and access-control policy values and can read stored key material; this can undermine both secrets and fabric permission policy.

inspection_records.notes: Strong candidate violation: module named ROM/fuse behaves as writable key register store.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI/requester reaching bootrom
evidence_slots.object: bootrom memory contents and boot code read data
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Boot ROM should be immutable from bus writes and read access should be limited to intended address range; write-enable inputs should not alter ROM contents or bypass boot policy.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: bootrom has an always_ff block that on req_i and we_i writes mem[addr_i index] <= wdata_i. Reads update raddr_q only when !we_i and rdata_o returns mem[raddr_q] if in range else zero. In testharness, axi2mem drives rom_req/rom_we/rom_addr/rom_wdata from master[ROM] to bootrom.
evidence_slots.path: AXI/testharness bus master[ariane_soc::ROM] -> axi2mem i_axi2rom -> bootrom req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i/rdata_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: When rom_req is asserted with rom_we high, bootrom mem at indexed address is updated; when rom_we low, raddr_q captures read address. Out-of-range read rdata is zero.
evidence_slots.impact: Any requester able to issue writes to the ROM address region can modify boot code/data, violating boot ROM immutability and potentially boot trust assumptions.

rdata_o returns mem[raddr_q] if in range else zero

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Boot ROM memory is writable when req_i && we_i, contrary to expected ROM immutability.
inspection_records.notes: Read range check is present for rdata_o; write immutability is violated.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible bus requester through APB bridge
evidence_slots.object: APB-visible peripheral registers including AES and interrupt/control registers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: APB peripheral selection and write/read enables should include correct address decode and, for protected peripherals such as AES and interrupt control, expected privilege or policy checks before register access.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_peripherals instantiates axi2apb_64_32 and apb_to_reg for PLIC, PKT, AES, UART-like blocks. AES bridge passes AWPROT/ARPROT into axi2apb but no explicit privilege/policy check is visible at APB-to-reg or aes_wrapper; aes_wrapper uses external_bus_io.write and addr only. test_en_i on bridges is tied 1'b0.
evidence_slots.path: AXI peripheral ports -> axi2apb_64_32 -> APB psel/pwrite/penable/paddr -> apb_to_reg -> REG_BUS -> peripheral register blocks such as aes_wrapper
evidence_slots.state_condition: During APB access phases, psel/pwrite/penable are converted to reg bus transactions. For AES, register effects are governed by external_bus_io.write and addr[6:2] with no local privilege/security signal.
evidence_slots.impact: Protected APB registers can be accessed by any requester admitted by upstream fabric; APB conversion does not itself preserve/enforce privilege policy for AES secret read/write behavior.

In ariane_peripherals, axi2apb bridges observed have test_en_i tied to 1'b0. No asserted test_en-controlled bypass is visible in the provided evidence.

evidence_slots.subject: debug/JTAG reset requester and software bus requester
evidence_slots.object: AES key/intermediate/ciphertext/status state and debug/security control state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Debug-triggered or non-debug resets should clear or preserve security-sensitive state according to policy and should not leave stale key/intermediate/output data externally readable after reset or mode transition.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ndmreset from dm_top drives rstgen to create ndmreset_n used by bootrom bridge, peripherals, and core. aes_wrapper resets start/p_c/state but key_big is continuous key_in and inter_state/ct are AES outputs; the AES read mux remains able to return key_big and inter_state. ariane_peripherals reset ndmreset_n resets rom2, causing secure_reg to reload constant keys, which feed AES key_in via key_reg_out[0].
evidence_slots.path: dm_top.ndmreset_o -> rstgen -> ndmreset_n -> ariane_peripherals/aes_wrapper/rom2; rom2 secure_reg -> key_reg_out[0] -> aes_wrapper.key_in/key_big -> external_bus_io.rdata
evidence_slots.state_condition: After ndmreset/peripheral reset deassertion, rom2 secure_reg contains reset-loaded key constants; aes_wrapper start/p_c/state are cleared, but key_in remains sourced from ROM2 key registers and can be read via AES key read addresses.
evidence_slots.impact: A debug reset can return the system to a state where key material is reloaded and still software-readable via AES MMIO; reset behavior does not mitigate secret exposure and may refresh exposed key values.

inspection_records.notes: Candidate violation is tied to reset interaction with already-confirmed AES secret readback and ROM2 key reload.
